page: Amazon Web Services r7g.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Graviton processor family with Neoverse-V1 cores running at 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. There is no local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data highlights strong memory subsystem performance. The server achieves a top-tier rating in the Passmark memory score and demonstrates strong uncached memory bandwidth, reaching 209.9 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 97.06 GB/sec for large-block write operations. CPU-bound workloads, such as stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests, perform in the average tier. However, cryptographic performance is a notable tradeoff, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the bottom 25 percent. LLM inference capabilities are moderate for small models but drop to the bottom 25 percent for prompt processing on medium 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 Graviton architecture delivers a highly competitive resource density for memory-bound workloads without the premium associated with traditional x86 architectures. Given the high memory capacity and strong memory bandwidth, this server is well-suited for memory-intensive applications such as open-source databases, Redis caching layers, in-memory analytics, and medium-scale web serving. It is less optimal for workloads requiring heavy cryptographic operations or local high-speed SSD storage.
description: The Amazon Web Services r7g.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by the ARM64 AWS Graviton processor family. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on the Neoverse-V1 architecture running at 2.6 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of system memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network speed of 8 Gbps but lacks local storage and GPU hardware. Benchmarks reveal top-tier memory performance, with strong uncached read and write memory bandwidth. However, cryptographic operations and medium-model LLM prompt processing represent performance tradeoffs, scoring in the lower percentiles. This server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive deployments, making it highly suitable for database operations, Redis caching, and in-memory data processing, while being less ideal for encryption-heavy tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.4xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.4xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server with 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized virtual server delivering high-bandwidth memory performance for caching, database operations, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 cores
Equipped with 128 GB of RAM providing 8 GB per core
Top-tier memory performance and strong uncached memory bandwidth
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for databases, Redis caching, and in-memory analytics
